The Python string_rev correctly reverses a string using slicing, matching the Coq recursive definition. The is_palindrome_spec in Python checks if result equals the boolean comparison of text with its reverse, which captures the same concrete behavior as the Coq biconditional (result = true <-> text = string_rev text) for boolean results.

The Python string_rev correctly reverses a string using slicing, matching the Coq recursive definition. The is_palindrome_spec in Python checks if result equals the boolean comparison of text with its reverse, which captures the same concrete behavior as the Coq biconditional (result = true <-> text = string_rev text) for boolean results.

The Python `string_rev` reverses a string just like the Coq `string_rev`. The spec in Coq says `result = true <-> text = string_rev text`, which for concrete boolean `result` is equivalent to `result = (text == string_rev(text))`, matching the Python implementation.
